title: Fix Cross-Tenant User Deletion Vulnerability
description:
**Penetration Test Finding — High Severity**

An admin of any tenant could delete users from a different tenant by intercepting the delete request and modifying the Team ID and User ID in the URL path (`/teams/members/{teamId}/{userId}`).

## Attack Vector

1. Login as Tenant A admin.
2. Navigate to Manage Account > select user > click Delete.
3. Intercept the request with Burp Suite.
4. Change Team ID and User ID to Tenant B values.
5. Tenant B user is deleted.

## Fix

The deletion endpoint now uses the currently authenticated user's team from the server session instead of the URL-supplied team_id. Even if the request is intercepted and modified, the server operates against the authenticated team context only.
acceptance_criteria: User deletion uses the server-side session team_id, not URL parameters
Modifying team_id in intercepted delete requests has no effect
Users can only be deleted from the authenticated user's own team
Cross-tenant user deletion is no longer possible
middleware_changes: User deletion endpoint refactored to derive team_id from the authenticated session context instead of URL path parameters.
api_changes: DELETE /teams/members/{teamId}/{userId} no longer trusts the teamId from the URL. Server derives it from the authenticated user's current team.
implementation_notes: Same pattern as MBT-425 fix. The deletion endpoint was using URL path parameters for team_id which could be manipulated. Fixed by deriving team_id from the authenticated session.
comment_summary: Developer fixed the issue by using the current authenticated team for deletion operations. Changes deployed to staging for review.
